vllm.title: [CI/Build] Enforce style for C++ and CUDA code with `clang-format`
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We have made great strides in enforcing code quality and consistency within our Python codebase, particularly with the recent MyPy integration. To maintain a high standard of code quality throughout the project, we should apply similar formatting and style guidelines to our C++ and CUDA code.

vllm.title: [Misc] Load FP8 kv-cache scaling factors from checkpoints
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The 2nd PR for #4532.
This PR supports loading FP8 kv-cache scaling factors from a FP8 checkpoint (with .kv_scale parameter).
Specifically,

We now support --kv-cache-dtype {auto, fp8, fp8_e4m3, fp8_e5m2}. auto=fp16 or bf16 and fp8=fp8_e4m3.
If the checkpoint is in FP16, then kv-cache scaling factor

vllm.title: [Core] Eliminate parallel worker per-step task scheduling overhead
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR replaces #3763.
Common logic is handled in the DistributedGPUExecutor superclass and used by both Ray and Mutliprocessing executors.

vllm.title: [Misc] Take user preference in attention selector
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The current selection logic on NVIDIA GPUs is a bit weird on NVIDIA GPUs. Specially, it checks whether the model can use FlashAttn, and directly falls back to xFormers if not, without considering consider user preference (i.e., environment variable VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND). User specified backend can

vllm.title: Marlin 24 prefill performance improvement (about 25% better on average)
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR introduces performance improvement to marlin_24's prefill (large batch) performance. The key fix is to ensure that the matmul instructions happen concurrently with the async global => shared memory load phase.
